folio.label: 119v
folio.l: Incipit fabula Armigeri

note.notetxt: 1There is a cross in dryed point next the starting point of the tale.ODR
1The ink in this folio is brown and becomes darker brown afterwards. NB an extra watermarked leafs was used here.ODR

folio.l: At Sarray , in the lond of Tartarye
There dwelled a kyngö , that werred Russye
Thorugú whicú there deyed , many a dougúty man
This noble kyngö was cleped , Cambyuscan
Whicú in his tyme , was of so gret renoun
That there was nowher , in no regiouö
So excellent a lord , of al thyngö
Hi~ lakked no thyngö , that longetú to a kyngö
As of the secte , of whicú that he was bornö
He kept his lay , to whicú that he was sworn~
And ther to he was hardy , wys and riche
And pitous and Iust , alwey I liche
Sootú of his word , benygne and honåable
Ad of his corage , alwey sad and stable
ñongö fressú and strongö , in armes desirous
As any bacheler , of al his hous
A faire ¹sone he was , and fortunat
And kept alwey so wel , Real estat
That there nas no wher , sucú an other man
¢ This noble kyngö , this Tartre Cambiuscan
Hadde two Sones , on Elfeta his wyf
Of whicú the eldest higút , Algarsyf
That other Sone , was called Camballo
A dougúter hadde , this worthy kyngö also
